original_hypothesis.title: Unit mismatch in PrizePool closeDraw distribution input may truncate contributions and shift accounting into reserve, creating claim shortfalls under high-liquidity conditions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: PrizePool.closeDraw passes uint96(_contributionsForDraw(lastClosedDrawId + 1)) into _nextDraw. If contributions exceed uint96 max, they truncate silently, causing the excess to remain in PrizePool token balance but not distributed into tiers/reserve as expected. This can desync prize distribution from actual contributed liquidity, causing unexpected reserve levels and potentially violating solvency expectations in edge high-volume environments.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While extreme, this is a code-level hard cap that can manifest as an operational incident during high TVL or unusual token decimal configurations. It requires scenario testing to confirm whether excess liquidity becomes permanently unaccounted or can be recovered via later accounting paths.
deployment_assumptions: Prize token decimals and expected contribution volumes could exceed type(uint96).max over a single draw after smoothing (e.g., very high TVL or low-decimal token with large nominal amounts)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: PrizePool.closeDraw casts contributions to uint96 (PrizePool.sol closeDraw); accumulator stores uint256.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Accumulator uses uint256 but closeDraw truncates to uint96 for distribution.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any draw with extremely large smoothed contributions.

operational_questions: What is the maximum plausible per-draw smoothed contribution under expected TVL/volume?
Does any subsequent accounting path ever incorporate the truncated excess into future draws/reserve?
Could this create situations where prizeToken.balanceOf is high but tiers/reserve remain low (unexpected behavior)?
validation_requirements: Stress-test accumulator contributions to exceed uint96 and observe post-closeDraw state changes
Verify whether _accountedBalance tracks the excess and whether claims/withdraws behave safely

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Flood PrizePool with prizeToken contributions attributed via contributePrizeTokens over one draw
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Ensure smoothed disbursement spikes above uint96 max
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Observe truncated distribution and resulting reserve/tier sizing anomalies
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attempt claims and measure whether unexpected reverts/shortfalls occur

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Incorrect prize sizing/reserve accounting; potential claim failures in extreme cases
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (may require contract upgrade/redeploy if hit)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Draw closure, prize sizing, reserve management

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Large gap between prizeToken.balanceOf(PrizePool) and distributed tier liquidity/reserve expectations
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: ReserveForOpenDraw estimates diverge from actual distribution outcomes
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Unusually large contributePrizeTokens events in single draw

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
